title: eriogonum parvifolium
intro: Eriogonum parvifolium, also known as the dwarf buckwheat or Oregon buckwheat, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain, inflammation, and improving immune function. Eriogonum parvifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
